instruction: Show me how to use TC39 Stage 3 decorators in TypeScript 5+ to implement method logging, caching, and validation patterns.
output:
<think>
TC39 Stage 3 decorators (supported in TypeScript 5.0+) are different from the legacy experimental decorators. The new API: a decorator is a function that receives (value, context) where value is the thing being decorated and context has metadata about it (kind, name, access, addInitializer, etc.). For methods, value is the function itself and you return a replacement function. For classes, value is the class constructor. Key differences from legacy: no property descriptors, no reflect-metadata, decorators run at class definition time, and metadata is stored on Symbol.metadata. The context.kind tells you what you're decorating: 'class', 'method', 'getter', 'setter', 'field', 'accessor'. I'll implement three practical decorators: @log for method tracing, @cache for memoization, and @validate for parameter validation.
</think>

Stage 3 decorators in TypeScript 5.0+:

```typescript
// tsconfig.json needs: "experimentalDecorators": false (use native)
// TypeScript 5.0+ uses Stage 3 decorators by default

// === Method Logging Decorator ===
function log<This, Args extends any[], Return>(
  target: (this: This, ...args: Args) => Return,
  context: ClassMethodDecoratorContext<This, (this: This, ...args: Args) => Return>
) {
  const methodName = String(context.name);

  return function (this: This, ...args: Args): Return {
    console.log(`[ENTER] ${methodName}(${args.map(a => JSON.stringify(a)).join(', ')})`);
    const start = performance.now();
    try {
      const result = target.call(this, ...args);
      const elapsed = (performance.now() - start).toFixed(2);
      console.log(`[EXIT] ${methodName} -> ${JSON.stringify(result)} (${elapsed}ms)`);
      return result;
    } catch (e) {
      console.log(`[ERROR] ${methodName} threw: ${e}`);
      throw e;
    }
  };
}

// === Caching Decorator ===
function cache<This, Args extends any[], Return>(
  target: (this: This, ...args: Args) => Return,
  context: ClassMethodDecoratorContext<This, (this: This, ...args: Args) => Return>
) {
  const cacheMap = new Map<string, Return>();

  return function (this: This, ...args: Args): Return {
    const key = JSON.stringify(args);
    if (cacheMap.has(key)) {
      return cacheMap.get(key)!;
    }
    const result = target.call(this, ...args);
    cacheMap.set(key, result);
    return result;
  };
}

// === Bound Decorator (auto-bind this) ===
function bound<This, Args extends any[], Return>(
  target: (this: This, ...args: Args) => Return,
  context: ClassMethodDecoratorContext<This, (this: This, ...args: Args) => Return>
) {
  const methodName = context.name;
  // addInitializer runs when the class is instantiated
  context.addInitializer(function (this: This) {
    (this as any)[methodName] = target.bind(this);
  });
  return target;
}

// === Field Validator Decorator ===
function validate(min: number, max: number) {
  return function <This>(
    target: undefined,  // Fields receive undefined as target
    context: ClassFieldDecoratorContext<This, number>
  ) {
    return function (this: This, initialValue: number): number {
      if (initialValue < min || initialValue > max) {
        throw new RangeError(
          `${String(context.name)}: ${initialValue} not in [${min}, ${max}]`
        );
      }
      return initialValue;
    };
  };
}

// === Accessor Decorator (auto getter/setter with validation) ===
function clamp(min: number, max: number) {
  return function <This>(
    target: ClassAccessorDecoratorTarget<This, number>,
    context: ClassAccessorDecoratorContext<This, number>
  ): ClassAccessorDecoratorResult<This, number> {
    return {
      get(this: This): number {
        return target.get.call(this);
      },
      set(this: This, value: number) {
        const clamped = Math.max(min, Math.min(max, value));
        target.set.call(this, clamped);
      },
      init(value: number): number {
        return Math.max(min, Math.min(max, value));
      },
    };
  };
}

// === Usage ===
class UserService {
  @validate(1, 1000)
  maxResults = 100;

  accessor @clamp(0, 100) opacity = 50;

  @log
  @cache
  findUser(id: number): { id: number; name: string } | null {
    // Expensive lookup...
    return { id, name: `User ${id}` };
  }

  @bound
  handleClick() {
    console.log('this is correctly bound:', this);
  }
}

const svc = new UserService();
svc.findUser(1);  // [ENTER] findUser(1) ... [EXIT] findUser -> {...}
svc.findUser(1);  // Cache hit — no log from inner function
svc.opacity = 150; // Clamped to 100
```

instruction: How do I implement a dependency injection container in TypeScript using Stage 3 decorators and the metadata API for automatic constructor injection?
output:
<think>
DI with Stage 3 decorators requires a different approach than the legacy reflect-metadata pattern. Stage 3 decorators have Symbol.metadata for storing metadata on decorated classes. The pattern: use a class decorator to mark injectable classes, use parameter metadata to identify dependencies (since Stage 3 doesn't have parameter decorators, we use a static property or a class decorator that reads constructor parameter types). The container registers types and resolves them recursively. Since TS Stage 3 decorators don't support parameter decorators yet, the common pattern is to use class decorators that register the class + field decorators or a tokens array. I'll implement a simple container with: @injectable class decorator, @inject field decorator, token-based registration, and recursive resolution with circular dependency detection.
</think>

DI container with Stage 3 decorators:

```typescript
// === DI Container Core ===
type Constructor<T = any> = new (...args: any[]) => T;
type Token<T = any> = symbol | string | Constructor<T>;

class Container {
  private bindings = new Map<Token, { factory: () => any; singleton: boolean }>();
  private instances = new Map<Token, any>();
  private resolving = new Set<Token>(); // Circular dependency detection

  register<T>(token: Token<T>, factory: () => T, singleton = true): this {
    this.bindings.set(token, { factory, singleton });
    return this;
  }

  registerClass<T>(token: Token<T>, ctor: Constructor<T>, singleton = true): this {
    this.bindings.set(token, {
      factory: () => this.createInstance(ctor),
      singleton,
    });
    return this;
  }

  resolve<T>(token: Token<T>): T {
    // Check singleton cache
    if (this.instances.has(token)) {
      return this.instances.get(token);
    }

    // Circular dependency check
    if (this.resolving.has(token)) {
      throw new Error(`Circular dependency detected for: ${String(token)}`);
    }

    const binding = this.bindings.get(token);
    if (!binding) {
      throw new Error(`No binding for: ${String(token)}`);
    }

    this.resolving.add(token);
    try {
      const instance = binding.factory();
      if (binding.singleton) {
        this.instances.set(token, instance);
      }
      return instance;
    } finally {
      this.resolving.delete(token);
    }
  }

  private createInstance<T>(ctor: Constructor<T>): T {
    // Read injection metadata from Symbol.metadata
    const meta = (ctor as any)[Symbol.metadata];
    const instance = new ctor();

    // Resolve field injections
    if (meta?.injections) {
      for (const [field, token] of Object.entries(meta.injections)) {
        (instance as any)[field] = this.resolve(token as Token);
      }
    }

    return instance;
  }
}

// === Decorators ===

// @injectable — registers class with the container
function injectable(token?: Token) {
  return function <T extends Constructor>(
    target: T,
    context: ClassDecoratorContext<T>
  ) {
    const resolveToken = token ?? target;
    // Register after class is fully defined
    context.addInitializer(function () {
      globalContainer.registerClass(resolveToken, target);
    });
    return target;
  };
}

// @inject — marks a field for injection
function inject(token: Token) {
  return function <This, V>(
    _target: undefined,
    context: ClassFieldDecoratorContext<This, V>
  ) {
    // Store injection metadata
    context.metadata ??= {} as any;
    const meta = context.metadata as any;
    meta.injections ??= {};
    meta.injections[String(context.name)] = token;

    // Return initializer that will be replaced by container
    return function (_initialValue: V): V {
      return undefined as V; // Placeholder, container fills it
    };
  };
}

// === Global container ===
const globalContainer = new Container();

// === Tokens for interfaces ===
const LOGGER = Symbol('Logger');
const CONFIG = Symbol('Config');

// === Usage ===
interface ILogger {
  log(msg: string): void;
}

interface IConfig {
  dbUrl: string;
}

@injectable(LOGGER)
class ConsoleLogger implements ILogger {
  log(msg: string) {
    console.log(`[LOG] ${msg}`);
  }
}

@injectable(CONFIG)
class AppConfig implements IConfig {
  dbUrl = 'postgres://localhost/app';
}

@injectable()
class UserRepository {
  @inject(LOGGER) logger!: ILogger;
  @inject(CONFIG) config!: IConfig;

  findAll() {
    this.logger.log(`Querying ${this.config.dbUrl}`);
    return [{ id: 1, name: 'Alice' }];
  }
}

// Resolution
const repo = globalContainer.resolve(UserRepository);
repo.findAll(); // [LOG] Querying postgres://localhost/app
```

instruction: Show me how to implement aspect-oriented programming patterns in TypeScript using decorators to add cross-cutting concerns like retry logic, rate limiting, and circuit breaking.
output:
<think>
AOP with decorators lets you separate cross-cutting concerns from business logic. Each concern (retry, rate limit, circuit breaker) becomes a decorator that wraps the original method. The key pattern: each decorator is a higher-order function that wraps the target method, adding behavior before/after/around the call. For composability, decorators should be stackable — @retry @rateLimit means retry wraps rateLimit wraps the original. The order matters: decorators apply bottom-up (closest to the method first). For async methods (common in real apps), the wrapper must handle both sync and async returns. I'll implement: @retry with exponential backoff, @rateLimit with token bucket, and @circuitBreaker with failure threshold and cooldown.
</think>

AOP decorators for resilience patterns:

```typescript
// === Retry with exponential backoff ===
function retry(options: { maxAttempts?: number; backoffMs?: number; retryOn?: Function[] } = {}) {
  const { maxAttempts = 3, backoffMs = 100, retryOn } = options;

  return function <This, Args extends any[], Return>(
    target: (this: This, ...args: Args) => Return,
    context: ClassMethodDecoratorContext
  ) {
    return async function (this: This, ...args: Args): Promise<Awaited<Return>> {
      let lastError: Error;

      for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++) {
        try {
          const result = await target.call(this, ...args);
          return result as Awaited<Return>;
        } catch (error) {
          lastError = error as Error;

          if (retryOn && !retryOn.some(cls => error instanceof cls)) {
            throw error; // Not a retryable error
          }

          if (attempt < maxAttempts) {
            const delay = backoffMs * Math.pow(2, attempt - 1);
            console.warn(
              `[retry] ${String(context.name)} attempt ${attempt}/${maxAttempts} ` +
              `failed: ${lastError.message}. Retrying in ${delay}ms`
            );
            await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, delay));
          }
        }
      }

      throw lastError!;
    } as any;
  };
}

// === Rate Limiter (Token Bucket) ===
function rateLimit(options: { requestsPerSecond: number }) {
  const { requestsPerSecond: rps } = options;
  let tokens = rps;
  let lastRefill = Date.now();

  return function <This, Args extends any[], Return>(
    target: (this: This, ...args: Args) => Return,
    context: ClassMethodDecoratorContext
  ) {
    return async function (this: This, ...args: Args): Promise<Awaited<Return>> {
      // Refill tokens
      const now = Date.now();
      const elapsed = (now - lastRefill) / 1000;
      tokens = Math.min(rps, tokens + elapsed * rps);
      lastRefill = now;

      if (tokens < 1) {
        const waitMs = ((1 - tokens) / rps) * 1000;
        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, waitMs));
        tokens = 0;
      } else {
        tokens -= 1;
      }

      return await target.call(this, ...args) as Awaited<Return>;
    } as any;
  };
}

// === Circuit Breaker ===
function circuitBreaker(options: {
  failureThreshold?: number;
  cooldownMs?: number;
} = {}) {
  const { failureThreshold = 5, cooldownMs = 30000 } = options;
  let failures = 0;
  let state: 'closed' | 'open' | 'half-open' = 'closed';
  let openedAt = 0;

  return function <This, Args extends any[], Return>(
    target: (this: This, ...args: Args) => Return,
    context: ClassMethodDecoratorContext
  ) {
    return async function (this: This, ...args: Args): Promise<Awaited<Return>> {
      if (state === 'open') {
        if (Date.now() - openedAt >= cooldownMs) {
          state = 'half-open'; // Allow one test request
        } else {
          throw new Error(
            `Circuit open for ${String(context.name)}. ` +
            `Try again in ${Math.ceil((cooldownMs - (Date.now() - openedAt)) / 1000)}s`
          );
        }
      }

      try {
        const result = await target.call(this, ...args) as Awaited<Return>;
        // Success: reset
        if (state === 'half-open') {
          console.log(`[circuit] ${String(context.name)} recovered`);
        }
        failures = 0;
        state = 'closed';
        return result;
      } catch (error) {
        failures++;
        if (failures >= failureThreshold || state === 'half-open') {
          state = 'open';
          openedAt = Date.now();
          console.error(
            `[circuit] ${String(context.name)} opened after ${failures} failures`
          );
        }
        throw error;
      }
    } as any;
  };
}

// === Usage: Stack decorators for resilient service calls ===
class PaymentService {
  // Order: circuitBreaker wraps retry wraps rateLimit wraps actual call
  @circuitBreaker({ failureThreshold: 3, cooldownMs: 10000 })
  @retry({ maxAttempts: 3, backoffMs: 200 })
  @rateLimit({ requestsPerSecond: 10 })
  async charge(userId: string, amount: number): Promise<{ txId: string }> {
    const response = await fetch(`/api/charge`, {
      method: 'POST',
      body: JSON.stringify({ userId, amount }),
    });
    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`Payment failed: ${response.status}`);
    return response.json();
  }
}
```

instruction: How do I use TypeScript decorators with metadata reflection to build a declarative validation framework where validation rules are defined via decorators on class fields?
output:
<think>
Stage 3 decorators include a metadata mechanism via Symbol.metadata. The pattern for validation: define field decorators that store validation rules in metadata, then a validate function that reads the metadata and checks each field. Since Stage 3 field decorators receive (undefined, context) and can use context.metadata to store information, we accumulate rules per field. The validate function then iterates over the metadata and checks the actual field values against the rules. Unlike legacy decorators + reflect-metadata, Stage 3 metadata is stored directly on the class via Symbol.metadata. I need to handle: required fields, string length, number range, pattern matching, custom validators, and nested object validation. The metadata structure maps field names to arrays of validation rules.
</think>

Declarative validation framework with Stage 3 decorator metadata:

```typescript
// === Validation Rule Types ===
interface ValidationRule {
  name: string;
  message: string;
  validate: (value: any) => boolean;
}

interface ValidationResult {
  valid: boolean;
  errors: { field: string; rule: string; message: string }[];
}

type FieldRules = Map<string | symbol, ValidationRule[]>;

// === Core: Store rules in metadata ===
function addRule(context: ClassFieldDecoratorContext, rule: ValidationRule) {
  const meta = (context.metadata as any);
  if (!meta._validationRules) {
    meta._validationRules = new Map() as FieldRules;
  }
  const rules = meta._validationRules as FieldRules;
  const fieldName = context.name;
  if (!rules.has(fieldName)) {
    rules.set(fieldName, []);
  }
  rules.get(fieldName)!.push(rule);
}

// === Decorator: @required ===
function required<This, V>(
  _target: undefined,
  context: ClassFieldDecoratorContext<This, V>
) {
  addRule(context, {
    name: 'required',
    message: `${String(context.name)} is required`,
    validate: (v) => v !== null && v !== undefined && v !== '',
  });
}

// === Decorator: @minLength / @maxLength ===
function minLength(min: number) {
  return function <This>(
    _target: undefined,
    context: ClassFieldDecoratorContext<This, string>
  ) {
    addRule(context, {
      name: 'minLength',
      message: `${String(context.name)} must be at least ${min} characters`,
      validate: (v) => typeof v === 'string' && v.length >= min,
    });
  };
}

function maxLength(max: number) {
  return function <This>(
    _target: undefined,
    context: ClassFieldDecoratorContext<This, string>
  ) {
    addRule(context, {
      name: 'maxLength',
      message: `${String(context.name)} must be at most ${max} characters`,
      validate: (v) => typeof v === 'string' && v.length <= max,
    });
  };
}

// === Decorator: @range ===
function range(min: number, max: number) {
  return function <This>(
    _target: undefined,
    context: ClassFieldDecoratorContext<This, number>
  ) {
    addRule(context, {
      name: 'range',
      message: `${String(context.name)} must be between ${min} and ${max}`,
      validate: (v) => typeof v === 'number' && v >= min && v <= max,
    });
  };
}

// === Decorator: @pattern ===
function pattern(regex: RegExp, msg?: string) {
  return function <This>(
    _target: undefined,
    context: ClassFieldDecoratorContext<This, string>
  ) {
    addRule(context, {
      name: 'pattern',
      message: msg ?? `${String(context.name)} does not match pattern ${regex}`,
      validate: (v) => typeof v === 'string' && regex.test(v),
    });
  };
}

// === Decorator: @custom ===
function custom(fn: (value: any) => boolean, message: string) {
  return function <This, V>(
    _target: undefined,
    context: ClassFieldDecoratorContext<This, V>
  ) {
    addRule(context, { name: 'custom', message, validate: fn });
  };
}

// === Validate Function ===
function validate<T extends object>(instance: T): ValidationResult {
  const ctor = instance.constructor as any;
  const meta = ctor[Symbol.metadata] as any;
  const rules = meta?._validationRules as FieldRules | undefined;

  if (!rules) return { valid: true, errors: [] };

  const errors: ValidationResult['errors'] = [];

  for (const [field, fieldRules] of rules) {
    const value = (instance as any)[field];
    for (const rule of fieldRules) {
      if (!rule.validate(value)) {
        errors.push({
          field: String(field),
          rule: rule.name,
          message: rule.message,
        });
      }
    }
  }

  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors };
}

// === Usage ===
class CreateUserRequest {
  @required
  @minLength(2)
  @maxLength(50)
  name: string = '';

  @required
  @pattern(/^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+$/, 'Invalid email format')
  email: string = '';

  @range(13, 150)
  age: number = 0;

  @custom(
    (v) => typeof v === 'string' && v.length >= 8 && /[A-Z]/.test(v) && /\d/.test(v),
    'Password must be 8+ chars with uppercase and digit'
  )
  password: string = '';
}

const req = new CreateUserRequest();
req.name = 'A';  // Too short
req.email = 'bad';
req.age = 5;     // Under 13
req.password = 'weak';

const result = validate(req);
console.log(result);
// { valid: false, errors: [
//   { field: 'name', rule: 'minLength', message: '...' },
//   { field: 'email', rule: 'pattern', message: '...' },
//   { field: 'age', rule: 'range', message: '...' },
//   { field: 'password', rule: 'custom', message: '...' },
// ]}
```
